repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

odd.head: General Note
odd.p: Forms part of the Rose Goldsen Archive of New Media Art.

odd.head: General Note
odd.p: See also: ETC digital collection, which consists of digitized videos from this collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Collection contains files related to programs and events sponsored or supported by ETC, grant proposals and reports, research files, publications from the ETC library, files on individual artists and institutions, taped interviews, and recorded conferences and workshops.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Experimental Television Center records, #8229. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Experimental Television Center (ETC) was founded in Owego, N.Y. in 1971 as an outgrowth of a media access program established by Ralph Hocking at Binghamton University in 1969, to provide support and services to the media arts community. ETC's mission was to encourage the creation of work using new electronic media technologies; to encourage an informed appreciation of media art; and to help honor our independently created moving-image heritage by initiating projects and participating in partnerships which address the needs for research, education and preservation, and place independent works within a larger cultural context.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is in the process of being transferred to its new owner. We no longer provide access to it.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Original arrangement. The folders are organized by program, report, activity, and correspondence, mostly chronological.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Boxes 60 and 61: Folder titles created by staff (Alana Staiti)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains promotional materials (flyers, posters, programs, press releases, articles) and planning materials (correspondence, reports, meeting minutes) related to the events that the Experimental Television Center (ETC) and other affiliated institutions (e.g. Visual Studies Workshop) have organized or participated in.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to folders in this series must be requested a week in advance.

Rappleyea to Sherry Miller about the successful acquisition of funds for NYSCA fiscal 1986-1987, April 15, 1986; Project description by Peer Bode, discusses his vision, inspiration, and views on electronic imaging, 1986; NYSCA Newsletter, Winter 1986 No. 3, announces Peer Bode's award for frame buffer construction and videotape making through ETC; Flyer from Media Alliance expressing urgent need to advocate for arts funding in New York, 1986, attached to a letter from Deborah Gee-Tritschler, Broome County Arts Council, to area arts organizations, no date; Handwritten letter from Shigeko Kubota to Sherry Miller and Ralph Hocking, mentioning Kubota's diary tape and Nam June Paik, February 20, 1986, and reply letter, presumably from Sherry Miller but not signed, March 17, 1986; Letter from Dai Sil Kim-Gibson, Director of Media Program NYSCA to Sherry Miller, regarding upcoming panel meeting and Minority Development Proposals, January 10, 1986; Correspondences from Reynold Weidenaar, 1985-1986; Handwritten letter from Jeff Bechtel to Sherry Miller, November 28, 1985; Series of correspondences from Sciarabba Walker & Co. Accountants, Ithaca NY to Ralph Hocking regarding audit for fiscal year 1985; Letter from Sherry Miller to Ben Day, Professor in the Department of Communication Arts and Design, Virginia Commonwealth University, regarding Walter Wright's tenure review, October 22, 1985; Letter from Robin F. White, Director of Media Alliance to Ralph Hocking and Sherry Miller regarding annual conference, curated screenings by Shalom Gorewitz, demonstrations by David Jones, August 9, 1985; Letters from S. Sengupta, Director of Sonex Private Limited, Calcutta, India, May 31, 1985, and Alberto Pascual Rodriguez, Production Assistant, Sociedad de Estudios de Radio y Television (SERTEL), postmarked 1985, requesting information about video production and training at ETC; Letter from assemblyman Matthew J. Murphy to Sherry Miller regarding securing additional funding for NYSCA for fiscal year 1986, March 19, 1986; Letter from Shalom Gorewitz to Sherry Miller and Ralph Hocking, regarding current projects using the Amiga and Commodore, undated.

scopecontent.p: Reading materials and course materials for Visual Studies Workshop course taught by Sherry Miller Hocking. scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains applications, budgets, reports, and correspondence related to granting agencies including New York State Council for the Arts (NYSCA) and the National Endowment for the Arts (NEA). These grants are used primarily for artist residency, program, event, and research. The series also contains materials related to ETC's own residency program, presentation fund, finishing fund, electronic arts grant, etc.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to folders in this series must be requested a week in advance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes bibliographies, syllabi, notes, manuscripts, interviews, and other sources related to a variety of research projects by Woody Vasulka, Steina Vasulka, Ralph Hocking, Sherry Miller Hocking, among others.

The subseries National Endowment for the Arts Funded Research Project in Box 117, 27-66; Box 1118, 1-8, consists of research projects supported by the National Endowment for the Arts. Jon Burris is the primary researcher.

did.unittitle: National Endowment for the Arts Funded Research Project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview. The research project concerned the design and creation of electronic moving-image instruments by independent artists and engineers, and the place of this work within the context of video art history. The project included original research, including audio interviews and the creation of original informational and other texts, and the compilation of existing descriptive materials. The notes on the audiotapes were written by Jon Burris, a researcher with the project. Stephen Beck interview.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents include Michael Mittelman short experimental videos and other media art. Each volume has its own theme. Volume 7 is called Personas and Personalities. It contains contributions from ten artists and focuses on the construction of identity and role-playing, along with the influence society and the mass media have on this construction. Aspect: The Chronicle of New Media Art.

Meetfactory and Banff Centre.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains audio and video tapes of interviews and conversations with various artists. Transcripts and notes are sometimes provided. It also contains ETC's telephone logs (31 spiral notebooks with telephone messages from various artists).

The material in Box 106-A, Experimental Television Center with the Vasulkas, Interviews by Jon Burris was created with a grant from the National Endowment for the Arts. The research project concerns the design and creation of electronic moving-image instruments by independent artists and engineers, and the place of this work within the context of video art history. The researchers include Woody Vasulka, Steina Vasulka, Ralph Hockings, Sherry Miller Hocking, and Jon Burris.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents include 31 spiral notebooks with telephone messages. Calls from various artists including Gary Hill, Barbara Buckner, Peter Campus, and Nam June Paik.

The concerts participants include Joel Chadabe, Judith Martin, Freida Schrott and the Buffalo String Quartet, Pat Feldballe and Walter Gajewski, Martin Kalve and Friends. controlaccess.geogname: Tisch School of the Arts, New York City, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In this subseries, materials are related to the conference Video History: Making Connections at Syracuse University. Lectures and discussions aimed to explore how this new medium of art, activism, and information was developed by artist innovators, video collectives, new technology manufacturers, funders, museum curators, and public television producers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The order of works is: 1. Grace, 2. Body, 3. Hours, 4. Night, 5. Lamb, 6. Landscape, 7. A Man, 8. Mese 9. The Blue, 10. Gathering In (note: beginning scene should read orange), 11. Axis, 12. Waters, 13. The Men, 14. A Table (note: beginning scene should read dark golden yellow), 15. Mast, 16. Image of the Kingdom, 17. Dominion; Older works: 18. Landscape. 19. Landscape, 20. Jewels of Air, 21. Rose, 22. View from Christabel
